payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: Three or four days from where you are now.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Should we go back down and just push the button, just make sure that we're wiped out once and for all? I'm kidding. We should make our way over to the Bisaft Isle, right? Need repairs?

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Yes.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: That would be the thing to do.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: We are pretty hurt here.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Yeah, how's everybody feeling, by the way? Are we damaged, beat up? We all right?

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Nothing that a good night's rest won't help.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: Or two... three.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: How many days will it be sailing?

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: I'd estimate probably three or four.

payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: To that degree, you guys have a few days of travel, anything you'd like to accomplish in this time? You've taken a long rest for the first evening. You're restored if you need to restore your spells and hit points.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Yes.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: That would be the thing to do.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: We are pretty hurt here.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Yeah, how's everybody feeling, by the way? Are we damaged, beat up? We all right?

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Nothing that a good night's rest won't help.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: Or two... three.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: How many days will it be sailing?

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: I'd estimate probably three or four.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Three or four days from where you are now.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: You got it right!

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Believe! You hear that? Holy shit, I got it right! As I was saying. Orly, if you would, please plot us a course to the Bisaft Isle.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "Of course, don't you worry." He goes ahead and sets course. The ship makes its turn across the water in the direction of the Bisaft Isle.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Bishaft?

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Bisaft.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: (singing) Bishaft, ah!

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: No H, it's Bisaft!

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: It's made of fucking cookies.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Bisaft.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Biscotti?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Yes.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: Biscotti Islands.

payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: Correct! Unless your cloak gives you advantage on dexterity saving throws?

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Two!

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: It's at an angle! Move your fucking thing!

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: It is at an angle.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: It's still a two, though. Plus?

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Plus seven, but wait! Don't I have something on dexterity saves where I can--

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: You get advantage?

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: I don't know. Don't I have something? I should know my shit!

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: You really should!

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: You have evasion.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: What's evasion? It's an area effect! So if it's a big explosion, maybe?

payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: Pre-Calamity is 800 years, and there's a whole era of very powerful magic--

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: There are no colors on deck that aren't associated with it. The only colors that are on deck were the colors that the ship previously sailed.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: We still have some fire damage on the boat, though? We're going to play that. Our flag was burned.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Our flag was burned by pirates.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: We could paint a new flag with your brush.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Yeah, but that's a really big amount of paint to use for a flag.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Make a history check, Caleb.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Okay. Are you ever going to use that paint?

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Yes, I am.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: You keep thinking on that sphere. You've read about similar objects, especially the conjuration aspect of it, that are used as focuses, similar to a bag of holding, to maintain and contain things for powerful mages. Its construction makes you feel like it's very old. Probably pre-Calamity.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Get the fuck out of here. What? That's so old.

payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: No, 800 years ago was the Calamity. Or a little over 800.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: We still have some fire damage on the boat, though? We're going to play that. Our flag was burned.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Our flag was burned by pirates.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: We could paint a new flag with your brush.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Yeah, but that's a really big amount of paint to use for a flag.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Make a history check, Caleb.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Okay. Are you ever going to use that paint?

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Yes, I am.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: You keep thinking on that sphere. You've read about similar objects, especially the conjuration aspect of it, that are used as focuses, similar to a bag of holding, to maintain and contain things for powerful mages. Its construction makes you feel like it's very old. Probably pre-Calamity.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Get the fuck out of here. What? That's so old.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Pre-Calamity is 800 years, and there's a whole era of very powerful magic--

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Is it only 800? I thought it was 8,000.

payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: From this point, a little over three days.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: I'll be showing Beau the ropes, first-manning the ship.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: All right, so you're getting that situated.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: I'll be finishing up some stuff in the kitchen. I'm going to run by Caleb one more time to have a chat about what he saw. Just mention, we've got two magical spheres on board. The one's like a clock thing--

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Maybe three.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: Technically three.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: One is in Fjord's gooch.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: I was thinking it's like a clock. Maybe that means time-- I don't know if time's passing, or something.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Oh, I'm very curious about it. I would like very much to see if she's willing to sell it or part with it somehow.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: Or at least let you take a look at it for a while or something. Maybe, I don't know. It gives me a chill. Not sure.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: I was not paying attention. Has anyone said how long it is until we reach land?

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: Three days.

payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: It's gone. It only lasts about a minute.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: (poof) That hole appears again.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Same size?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Same size, in a slightly different location.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Nott!

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: What?

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Do you want to go in a hole?

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: (gasps) What if the hole disappears while Nott is still in it?

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: I'm sure it'll be fine.

preceding_turns.speaker: DEBORAH
preceding_turns.text: I'll take out my lantern, and I strike it so that it's lit. I attach it to a string. I go and I start to lower it down the hole and see if it does anything.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: As the lantern hits the darkness, the darkness consumes it like water. Once the lantern is submerged within the darkness, no light. You don't even see the lantern.

preceding_turns.speaker: DEBORAH
preceding_turns.text: Then if I pull it up?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Still lit.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: I feel like we should put our head through it.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: I think so, too, but you've got to do it quick because it's going to stop real quick.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: It's still a foot?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Mm-hmm.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Do it, do it!

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: But do it really fast!

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: I'm not doing that, no way! You're crazy!

payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: Each time it's lasted about a minute, yeah.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: I'm sure it'll be fine.

preceding_turns.speaker: DEBORAH
preceding_turns.text: I'll take out my lantern, and I strike it so that it's lit. I attach it to a string. I go and I start to lower it down the hole and see if it does anything.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: As the lantern hits the darkness, the darkness consumes it like water. Once the lantern is submerged within the darkness, no light. You don't even see the lantern.

preceding_turns.speaker: DEBORAH
preceding_turns.text: Then if I pull it up?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Still lit.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: I feel like we should put our head through it.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: I think so, too, but you've got to do it quick because it's going to stop real quick.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: It's still a foot?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Mm-hmm.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Do it, do it!

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: But do it really fast!

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: I'm not doing that, no way! You're crazy!

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: It's gone. It only lasts about a minute.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: You could have been decapitated, doing that.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Yes! Thank you.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Well, he says it lasts a minute.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Yeah, it's been about twice, a minute each time.

payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: Both times, it's been approximately the same length of time, close to a minute.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: As the lantern hits the darkness, the darkness consumes it like water. Once the lantern is submerged within the darkness, no light. You don't even see the lantern.

preceding_turns.speaker: DEBORAH
preceding_turns.text: Then if I pull it up?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Still lit.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: I feel like we should put our head through it.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: I think so, too, but you've got to do it quick because it's going to stop real quick.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: It's still a foot?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Mm-hmm.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Do it, do it!

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: But do it really fast!

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: I'm not doing that, no way! You're crazy!

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: It's gone. It only lasts about a minute.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: You could have been decapitated, doing that.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Yes! Thank you.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Well, he says it lasts a minute.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Yeah, it's been about twice, a minute each time.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Each time it's lasted about a minute, yeah.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: When we've timed it!

payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: It takes a few minutes. This is an intricate lock. As you watch the hand finish with the third tool that is being held between the mystical pinkie, you hear a slight clink, like a spring snapped or moved. The front of the door here bursts into flame, burning out in the area. It burns solid for a good 30 seconds. Surrounded by flame, all of you are being lit by this. You're wincing from the heat you feel you should be feeling, but it's all being held at bay by the hut's boundaries. Eventually, the flames die out, the door, tapestry both untouched.

preceding_turns.speaker: DEBORAH
preceding_turns.text: Should the Mage Hand try to open it, you guys? I can send my hand to try and open it.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: We are still safe in here. Yeah, I think so.

preceding_turns.speaker: DEBORAH
preceding_turns.text: Captain Fjord?

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Proceed.

preceding_turns.speaker: DEBORAH
preceding_turns.text: Sweet! I whisper, and I direct it a little bit. It comes down, and it grabs a knob? A handle?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: There is a handle there.

preceding_turns.speaker: DEBORAH
preceding_turns.text: A handle, and it's going to turn.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: (clicking) It's locked.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Hmm. Ooh! Fjord, look in your pockets!

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Why?

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Of the red robe! Look in the pockets of the red robe!

preceding_turns.speaker: DEBORAH
preceding_turns.text: I call it back to me. I pull out of my purse a little set of tools on a ring, and I give it to the hand and whisper it off again to go see if it can pick the lock.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: The hand hovers across. Roll an ability check, adding your dexterity modifier and your proficiency modifier.

preceding_turns.speaker: DEBORAH
preceding_turns.text: My thieves' tools? That's a 29.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Whoa!

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: I don't have to do shit. I'm just going to sit there, drinking.

payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: All right, I need you guys to roll initiative.

preceding_turns.speaker: DEBORAH
preceding_turns.text: While Twiggy does this, she sticks her tongue out the side of her mouth and one eye closed.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: It appears to be unlocked.

preceding_turns.speaker: DEBORAH
preceding_turns.text: Okay.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Let's open it.

preceding_turns.speaker: DEBORAH
preceding_turns.text: We both take each one and open up the cabinet.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Okay, you open the inside of the cabinet, and it is empty.

preceding_turns.speaker: DEBORAH
preceding_turns.text: I don't buy it.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Let's feel around, wiggle around.

preceding_turns.speaker: DEBORAH
preceding_turns.text: We stick our hands in.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Okay! You put your hands inside the empty cabinet, and it immediately closes on your arms as teeth suddenly emerge from the sides of the door. (yelling, groaning)

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Shut up!

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: No, actually, this is an attack on each of you. Oh, that's cocked. Okay, that is 23 against you.

preceding_turns.speaker: DEBORAH
preceding_turns.text: Okay.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: And that is 17 against you.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Hits.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: All right. Each of you suffer 14 points of piercing damage.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Uncanny dodge.

preceding_turns.speaker: DEBORAH
preceding_turns.text: Yeah, uncanny dodge.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Both of you take seven points of piercing damage, but you are both currently grappled as it is slammed on your arms in a very The Thing kind of way, like when the guy's chest opens and closes. Yeah, it's some John Carpenter. It closes on you and you see on each of the doors where the striations of the wood are, they split and you see these eyes peek through at you.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: I immediately turn around and Invoke Duplicity down on the ground near them.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Okay. All right, that Invoke Duplicity appears. Where do you want her to be?

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Right next to them.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Okay, yeah, so you guys are both up here right now.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Mm-hmm.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: For Pete's-- Sacred Flame.

payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: Taking a minute to glance through, a couple of things. One, you pull open one of the drawers. You see a glass jar about this big that contains about a hundred or so of these small glass beads. There's that. You go, "Okay, that's that." On the desk itself, there's a large vial of ink, about that big. It's a heavy vial of ink with this big red quill placed next to it into a little cap.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: You've had your turn since, so you have your reaction back.

preceding_turns.speaker: DEBORAH
preceding_turns.text: So I can halve it now.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Yes, you can. So it would be 16 points of piercing damage, which is reduced to eight; however, you are swallowed.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Oh, she's in it!

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: So you're inside of it now. It scoops you up. It lowers down and slams the cabinet shut as it pulls back. Twiggy's legs are kicking there, and like Audrey 2, it just sucks her inside of the cabinet.

preceding_turns.speaker: DEBORAH
preceding_turns.text: I'll never stop loving you!

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: It's okay, she's lived in a box for like a week.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: She's used to this! Caleb, you're up.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: I am going to trust my friends to take care of the monster. I have a feeling about the desk. This is what I would do. I would think that there might be something in this man's desk, so I start looking for anything of use.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Make an investigation check.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: The desk is a mimic.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: It is a 16.

payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: Looking around, no. You see the smooth glass of different colors, and you see the seams that hold them together where it's been sealed. You can see the various colors and shapes around it. Nah. You're panicking a bit.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: Let's take a look at these.

preceding_turns.speaker: DEBORAH
preceding_turns.text: Do you want me to take a look at it?

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: Let's look at this lock, see if it seems trapped or something, or I don't know.

preceding_turns.speaker: DEBORAH
preceding_turns.text: Yeah, I'll check it for a trap.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Yeah, go for it. Make an investigation check.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: They do not appear to be trapped, but they are definitely locked.

preceding_turns.speaker: DEBORAH
preceding_turns.text: Okay. Thieves' tools?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: These bronze clasps around their form. Is that for one book?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: One book. You try and get there, and the lock breaks. One of your tools snaps inside.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: Here, wipe it on this. That's okay.

preceding_turns.speaker: DEBORAH
preceding_turns.text: I tried.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: There's the second book and the third book.

preceding_turns.speaker: DEBORAH
preceding_turns.text: Okay, second book? 21.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: That book, the metal brace opens up, and the book is available to you.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: Hey, Caleb!

preceding_turns.speaker: DEBORAH
preceding_turns.text: Caleb, we got books!

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Are there any sigils or writing anywhere on the frame of the window or in the window?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Make a perception check.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Balls. Five.

payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: The falchion appears. At which point, the head curls back and one of the arms (loud impact). You hear coins scatter as the impact sends bits of its loot across the stone floor and into the shadows around. "Ah. It's been a while since my food has played back."

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: You're not entirely certain where the pile of loot and the creature's body really begins. It's partially submerged into this nest that it's burrowed itself into. Besides from its neck that slowly begins to extend as its head lifts to meet your height, you can't really see the rest of it. It looks you over, its eyes taking in your shape. "Where's the fun in that? Why are you here, little morsel?"

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Can I be entirely honest with you?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "Oh, please."

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: I have recently been putting my hands where they don't belong. This time it sent me to you.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "And what have we learned?"

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Probably to look and not touch.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "Good. Tell me, what else have those grubby hands touched? I like to know the flavors to expect."

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Well, you know I do have a very salty variety myself. I'll summon the falchion in my hand.

payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: It turns its head curiously towards you and breathes. As it does, you watch these crackling blue bolts between its teeth, arcing between the jaws. It opens up slightly, the tongue running itself over the front. "I don't know where he walks these days. It's been a while, but we have an arrangement."

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: You can't even focus. Your eyes blur for a moment.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Yeah, small tears.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: You got the jake legs.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: It stands there and goes, "Halas has not returned in an age. You shall keep me entertained for now."

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: That's funny, I've actually heard that name before, Halas. We saw some of his belongings. Before you devour me like a tiny little shrimp, would you mind a small request in letting me know who that person was?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Make a persuasion check.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: I am charismatic. (squealing) 23! Natural 19.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Still standing, you glance over your shoulder and you can see now its form. It's maybe, from wing tip to wing tip, about 20 or so feet. The body itself is not incredibly massive. This is a younger dragon, but it's still a fucking dragon.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Cool when there are six others, seven others. (panicked mumbling)

payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: "Yes. I keep watch on some of his surroundings and he sends me food and gifts. One day I'll be big enough to eat him and this will all be mine. But until then, I'm fine with the flattery."

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Make a persuasion check.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: I am charismatic. (squealing) 23! Natural 19.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Still standing, you glance over your shoulder and you can see now its form. It's maybe, from wing tip to wing tip, about 20 or so feet. The body itself is not incredibly massive. This is a younger dragon, but it's still a fucking dragon.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Cool when there are six others, seven others. (panicked mumbling)

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: It turns its head curiously towards you and breathes. As it does, you watch these crackling blue bolts between its teeth, arcing between the jaws. It opens up slightly, the tongue running itself over the front. "I don't know where he walks these days. It's been a while, but we have an arrangement."

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: An arrangement, you and he?

payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: Well, let's roll initiative first.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: He thinks for a second to himself. "Time does move strangely chamber to chamber here. If he is, well, I guess that makes me lord of the manor."

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Oh no.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: You hear another-- (footsteps)

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Manor? Are we in a house?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "More or less." (footsteps) "If you can call the hundred or so chambers here a house."

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Jeez. You know, I hate to leave you without a friend. I mean, if this guy's up and left you high and dry, maybe I could offer my services.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Make a persuasion check. Roll fucking high.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: He goes, "Huh. I appreciate the offer, but I tend to work solo and let's be honest, I'm hungry." There are a few flashes from the center of the above hallway as a few bodies fall through. Beau and Jester both come falling through, Bill and Ted style. Both of you guys make acrobatics checks for me.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: I was trying to draw that shit out--

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Natural 20!

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: You three-point land.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: You're good! 12!

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: You three-point land. (bones cracking) (screams)

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: You have no idea how happy I am to see you. Dragon!

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Five point of bludgeoning damage to you. As you land, the dragon rears up, caught off guard, wings up in the air. I'm going to pull the map out. (cheering)

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Oh my god. D&D Beyond.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: Oh boy.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: This feels like a lot.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Damn it. Ooh!

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: Turn it around this way. Oh boy.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: For this, Jester landed there. Fjord-- sorry, Beau is prone there.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: I started to take a step to the left while facing the dragon.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: And the dragon is right there.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Okay. The minute he rears up, I'm going to Invoke Duplicity again.

payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: Yeah, it is concentration, so you're considered concentrating right now. It's friendly to you and your companions. Roll initiative for the elemental.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: No. Blink was my action.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Correct, but at the end of each of your turns--

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Oh, I do. I roll. Yep. That is 11.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: On 11 or higher, you vanish from the current plane. That's where you were. You disappear. That ends your go. Beau, you're up. You're on the ground. Looking around, there's a dragon there. Fjord turns this way and vanishes. There's two of these darkened hallways. What are you doing?

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: I turn to Jester and I go  Fjord just fucking vanished again! And I go: I don't know how this works! I take out the red elemental gem that I've had for a very long time, and I... use it. I was going to say I smash it, but then I felt that might be deeply irresponsible because then it could just smash.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Maybe you can punch him with the gem.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Punch the gem at him.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Badminton.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Red elemental gem. A fire elemental is summoned as if you cast the Conjure Elemental spell, and the gem's magic is lost. So as you throw it, it shatters. I don't have a fire elemental thing on me, so we'll use this for the time being. As you throw it, it breaks on the ground there. Suddenly, rising from the impact, the flames swirl and build up into a full-fledged fire elemental beneath.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: I go  Fuck him up!

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: As for the Conjure Elemental spell, let's see if it gets its action right now.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Just hope it listens to what you say.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: God, well, I think it's one of those things where, if something bad happens, it turns on us.

preceding_turns.speaker: DEBORAH
preceding_turns.text: I think it does. I think it's concentration.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Yeah, if I lose concentration, but I don't know--

payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: Yeah, so we'll say both of you guys drop. Both of you roll initiative. First of all, acrobatics check.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: It would kill me.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: (screaming) Oh, Matthew!

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Why would you do this to Deborah Ann Woll?

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: I'm sorry I touched the window!

preceding_turns.speaker: DEBORAH
preceding_turns.text: Deborah Ann Woll has a plan! Get to where I fall in! I'm still in inter-dimensional space!

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: You said 57 damage?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Correct.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: 52 damage?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: 52 damage. No, I'm sorry, 55.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Maybe we can give it the rotten drug fruit that we have.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: It's going to then fly back around to get a better area. This is actually going to take up in the air.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: How high is the ceiling in here?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: The ceiling is 20 feet, so it gets to max at the very top. It's ten feet above anybody, so it's out of melee range unless somebody can get to the top.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Wow. This is bad.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Boom! Ah!

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: I have evasion. That's the only reason I'm okay.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Wait, that seems much higher than 20 feet.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: It's 20 feet. It's right there.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: It's hovering above. Thanks.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: The mini looks like it's flying when it's not. Now it's flying. It's at the base level.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: I'm with you now.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: The fire elemental does get an attack of opportunity as it moves out of its range. Shrugs it off.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Okay. Yes, it hits. I rolled a 17.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: 17 just misses.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: No, a natural 17 plus six.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: It does hit. Roll damage.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: 17 misses?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: The flames are burning around its body. It doesn't even pay attention.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Get me the fuck out of here. We're level seven. Why did we do this?

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: You did it!

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Ten damage.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Ten damage. Not bad. That's top of the round, so (approaching screams). Next up, I believe, was it Caduceus first?

preceding_turns.speaker: DEBORAH
preceding_turns.text: Did you go first, and then I went after you?

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: I think we went-- we may have--

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: In the same round.

preceding_turns.speaker: DEBORAH
preceding_turns.text: Same round, yeah.

payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: Yeah, it's all good. That finishes your go, Jester. It's now the dragon's turn.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: How would you know?

preceding_turns.speaker: DEBORAH
preceding_turns.text: How would you know? It's so dramatic!

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: First one, yep, it hits with a 20, which is nine points of damage. Sorry!

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: As the elemental slams the tiny weasel, the weasel reverts to the dragon form.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: And he gets another attack. Oh no, wait, he can only hold one attack.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Yeah, not the player. Well, no, I'll let you guys do all of your attacks, but I'll let the enemies do it, too.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: He hits with this one, as well.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: All right, roll damage.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: I'm sorry!

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: It's okay. That's the thing you have to consider with Polymorph. You have to decide if it's going to take damage if you're going to put it in a tiny form.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Yeah, I didn't hear--

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: It's all good.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: 11 points of damage. That was an awesome maneuver.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: So good.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: I mean, I shouldn't have fucking held his attack.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: How would you know?

payload.raw_text: Yep, for the next hour, the elemental is considered an enemy.

preceding_turns_last5.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns_last5.text: The fire elemental, right now, is no longer under Beau's control. The thread has been severed that was keeping it controlled as an elemental.

preceding_turns_last5.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns_last5.text: There's that whole thing about them which I kind of knew going into this. Sorry. Bye!

preceding_turns_last5.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns_last5.text: Yasha's just like looking around the boat.

payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: Yep, for the next hour, the elemental is considered an enemy.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Do I still have an action?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Yeah, that was just your movement.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Can I try and replicate on this sphere the movements I watched Twiggy do to get us here?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Sure. You reach out and touch the sphere (whooshing), Beau's gone.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: No! Fucking--

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Bye!

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Hope it's home.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: "Hope it's home."

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: That's your turn. Twiggy, you're up.

preceding_turns.speaker: DEBORAH
preceding_turns.text: Oh boy!

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: The fire elemental, right now, is no longer under Beau's control. The thread has been severed that was keeping it controlled as an elemental.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: There's that whole thing about them which I kind of knew going into this. Sorry. Bye!

preceding_turns.speaker: DEBORAH
preceding_turns.text: Shit.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Yasha's just like looking around the boat.

payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: It is. It is going to attempt to attack each of you once, since you're both within his attacking. Fjord, that is a 17 to hit?

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: No, no, no.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: It's just a dragon and a fire elemental. Yeah, take your time.

preceding_turns.speaker: DEBORAH
preceding_turns.text: What am I going to do? Okay, I'm going to-- I can use it-- oh, I already used my bonus action. Damn it, that's why I didn't want to do it. All right, I run to the pedestal, but I don't touch the thing.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Okay. 25. You're right there.

preceding_turns.speaker: DEBORAH
preceding_turns.text: Yeah, I'm right there. Okay.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Okay, that ends your go. That brings us to the fire elemental's turn. The fire elemental does not know where you are. It's going to move to the closest target. It's actually going to, as part of its fire form--

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: Is the dragon the closest to it? I mean--

preceding_turns.speaker: DEBORAH
preceding_turns.text: It would attack anything, right?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: The fire elemental is going to move through both of you. As it bursts through, you both burst into flames. So both of you are on fire.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Oh, sorry. It's half-dead.

payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: I'll allow that.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: To get there, yes.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: But people are still fucking down, aren't they?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Well, people are in a couple of rough places, and you're still missing Caleb and Nott. Up to you. You still have an action to use.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: Fudge. I'm going to do something dumb. Don't leave a man behind.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: I like dumb.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: I'm going to-- this is going to get weird. Halfway through my run, actually right before I start my run, I'm going to cast Meld into Stone.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Meld into Stone.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: You're going to what?

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: He's going to Blues Traveler it.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: All right. It's not concentration.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: It's not concentration. Casting Meld into Stone so that when I end my turn, in theory, I will not be on fire any more because I'll be inside of the stone.

payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: It's a lie-and-wait type of spell. Like merge into it and stay there for up to eight hours, and then once you leave it. But the fire's out and you have your movement.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Meld into Stone.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: You're going to what?

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: He's going to Blues Traveler it.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: All right. It's not concentration.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: It's not concentration. Casting Meld into Stone so that when I end my turn, in theory, I will not be on fire any more because I'll be inside of the stone.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: I'll allow that.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: Thank you.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: The flame goes out because there's no air for it to breathe. Do you step into it here?

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: Yeah, I'm actually just going to walk--

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: He Homer Simpsons into the stone wall.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: I go and I'm going to take my 30 feet straight towards the wall. I can't see anything, but I can move.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: You can use your movement to leave the stone where you entered it, which ends the spell. You don't get to move through the stone.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: I'm going to walk all the way.

payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: Jester remains. It's now the dragon's turn.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: But I wouldn't-- yeah.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: You also take one point of fire damage at the top of the round because you're still on fire.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Okay. I mean, this is so stupid. I'm going to cast Cure Wounds on myself because I'm going to die if I don't, at, I guess-- a couple of 3rd-levels left, so I'm going to cast Cure Wounds. Oh good! Two ones and a two. That's great. So four plus four is eight points healed.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Very valuable.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Sure.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: You've got to run. Just run.

preceding_turns.speaker: DEBORAH
preceding_turns.text: Dash and take it? Oh, you need a distraction.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Run and pray?

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: I mean, yeah. I'm going to run around as much as I can.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Okay. I mean, there's no way to--

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Around the edge of-- without getting hit, without me leaving his melee.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Yeah, so (counting) 30, you can get to.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Then Blink.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Roll for your d20.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: Oh god, come on.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: No. I stay.

payload.trigger_phrase: makes their way out of the
payload.raw_text: Help put it out, for those who are still burning in flames, you find yourselves back in the hull of the ship. You see the crew, half of the crew is sitting there, looking intense, the sphere resting on the center of one of the tables off to the side, and as soon as the first of you emerge, they're like, (starts) "Where have you been?! What's going on?" Orly pushes forward and goes, "Where all you been hanging?" (whooshing) A few more appear. (impacts) Eventually, everyone makes their way out of the sphere.

preceding_turns_last5.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns_last5.text: She could have grabbed him, worst case scenario.

preceding_turns_last5.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns_last5.text: As you all, one by one, emerge, beaten, on fire--

payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: Marius is like, "We've been waiting here for seven days."

preceding_turns.speaker: DEBORAH
preceding_turns.text: You guys, I did it! I killed the dragon all by myself!

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: What?

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: No, the dragon was alive, we just left.

preceding_turns.speaker: DEBORAH
preceding_turns.text: Aww, man. (laughter)

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Did you really?

preceding_turns.speaker: DEBORAH
preceding_turns.text: I really did.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Aww, Jessie...

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Yeah, roll that shit, Caduceus.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: Goddamn. Way to go, that's-- Did you get anything from him?

preceding_turns.speaker: DEBORAH
preceding_turns.text: I really tried to take a tooth, but I figured it'd be better to live.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: Yeah, aw man.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: You hear a clattering sound, and you glance down and you see, stuck in the side of your boot, what looks like one of the errant dragon tooths. (cheering)

preceding_turns.speaker: DEBORAH
preceding_turns.text: I take it and I put it in my bag, and I go: This'll be good for later.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: I'm going to go over to Jester and take the flower out of her head and be like: I think it worked. I'm going to take it back now.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: I turn and give Nott the biggest hug, just bury my head in her.

payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: "We're at Bisaft. Our repairs finished like a day ago and we haven't paid yet because you've been fucking gone! The Dockmaster's not happy!"

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: No, the dragon was alive, we just left.

preceding_turns.speaker: DEBORAH
preceding_turns.text: Aww, man. (laughter)

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Did you really?

preceding_turns.speaker: DEBORAH
preceding_turns.text: I really did.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Aww, Jessie...

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Yeah, roll that shit, Caduceus.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: Goddamn. Way to go, that's-- Did you get anything from him?

preceding_turns.speaker: DEBORAH
preceding_turns.text: I really tried to take a tooth, but I figured it'd be better to live.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: Yeah, aw man.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: You hear a clattering sound, and you glance down and you see, stuck in the side of your boot, what looks like one of the errant dragon tooths. (cheering)

preceding_turns.speaker: DEBORAH
preceding_turns.text: I take it and I put it in my bag, and I go: This'll be good for later.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: I'm going to go over to Jester and take the flower out of her head and be like: I think it worked. I'm going to take it back now.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: I turn and give Nott the biggest hug, just bury my head in her.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Marius is like, "We've been waiting here for seven days."

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Oh, shit.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: What?! Are you serious?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "Yeah!"

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Where are we?
